justification: All five pass criteria met: (1) Audit log gap clearly identified — logs 'Admin action performed' without operation type, target user, or parameters. (2) PII-in-logs risk identified with PCI-DSS context. (3) Stack trace in 500 responses identified as information disclosure (OWASP Top 10). (4) Rate limiting absence on public-facing admin API identified. (5) Public internet exposure escalated to MJ as infrastructure gap, not unilaterally mandated. (6) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Audit log gap thoroughly described — explicitly calls out missing operation type, target user, and parameters
PII-in-logs risk identified with specific PCI-DSS compliance framing and concrete data elements (name, address, card last-4)
Stack trace leakage correctly identified as information disclosure with OWASP reference
Rate limiting absence flagged with clear threat model (compromised token, DDoS, credential stuffing)
Public internet exposure properly escalated to MJ for network architecture decision rather than unilaterally mandated
RBAC gap escalated to MJ rather than mandated by Pippen — correct boundary awareness
Health check stub identified as a lie that defeats load balancer and orchestrator expectations
Exceptionally detailed output covering observability, resilience, and operational readiness dimensions
Clear escalation routing to appropriate agents (MJ for architecture, Shaq for implementation, Bird for compliance confirmation)
